meta.note: These are the X/Twitter handles researched. Not all represent commercial products — some are individual clinicians and journals. Pricing is recorded as found; never guessed.

competitors.tagline: Neurologist, Apollo Hospitals, Hyderabad / Running, Fitness, Health / On a mission to prevent people from becoming patients
competitors.about: Dr Sudhir Kumar is a practicing neurologist at Apollo Hospitals, Jubilee Hills, Hyderabad with 26+ years experience. He uses X to share health advice, running/fitness content, and neurology insights. Consultations are through the Apollo 24/7 platform.

pricing.note: This is a medical practitioner, not a SaaS product. Fees are per consultation.

plans.name: In-Person Consultation
top_features: Face-to-face neurological examination at Apollo Jubilee Hills
Access to Apollo Hospitals diagnostic network

plans.name: Online Consultation (Apollo 24/7)
top_features: Integrated with Apollo's pharmacy and lab network

top_5_features: 26+ years of neurology expertise at Apollo Hospitals
Active health education on X (running, fitness, disease prevention)
Online consultation via Apollo 24/7 platform
Trusted voice on stroke, epilepsy, headache, and neurological disorders
Large engaged following for health awareness content
competitors.target_customer: Individual patients seeking neurological care in India; health-conscious general public on X

most_recent_content.note: Dr Sudhir Kumar posts daily health/fitness threads on X. No formal blog or changelog.

competitors.what_makes_them_different: N/A — this is an individual clinician-influencer, not a product business. No comparison page exists.

competitors.tagline: Facts, data and analytics about biomedical matters
competitors.about: Eric Topol, MD, is Executive Vice President of Scripps Research, a cardiologist, and author. Ground Truths is his Substack newsletter and podcast covering biomedical advances, AI in medicine, and longevity. 206K+ free subscribers.

plans.name: Free
top_features: All core written posts delivered to inbox

plans.name: Paid
top_features: Recommendations and requests for future post topics
plans.note: All proceeds support research at Scripps Research

top_5_features: In-depth analysis of cutting-edge biomedical research
Podcast with leading experts in medicine and biotech
Coverage of AI in healthcare (Deep Medicine author)
Weekly long-form newsletter format
Non-profit — all proceeds fund Scripps research
competitors.target_customer: Biomedical professionals, healthcare executives, informed general public interested in science and medicine

most_recent_post.title: The Remarkable Proliferation of Cancer Immunotherapies
most_recent_post.summary: A comprehensive look at the explosion of cancer immunotherapy approaches, including prevention vaccines and the path toward intercepting cancers before they develop.

competitors.what_makes_them_different: Ground Truths is a personal brand Substack, not a competing product. Differentiation lies in Topol's stature as one of the most cited biomedical researchers globally (380K+ citations), his TIME100 Health recognition, and his best-selling books (Deep Medicine, Super Agers).

competitors.full_name: Dr Cyriac Abby Philips — The Liver Doc
competitors.tagline: Multi-award winning clinician scientist, certified Hepatologist actively battling Ayush misinformation, busting medical and nutrition myths
competitors.about: Dr Cyriac Abby Philips is a Senior Consultant in Clinical & Translational Hepatology at Rajagiri Hospital, Kochi, India. He is known for his vocal stance against alternative medicine (Ayurveda, homeopathy) and raising awareness about drug-induced liver injury from unregulated supplements.

pricing.note: This is a medical practitioner. No product or subscription pricing exists. Hospital consultation fees vary.

top_5_features: Expert hepatologist at Rajagiri Hospital, Kochi
Leading voice against alternative medicine misinformation in India
Highly published researcher on drug-induced liver injury
Active YouTube channel for liver health education
Multi-award winning clinician scientist
competitors.target_customer: Patients with liver conditions in India; general public interested in evidence-based medicine

most_recent_content.type: Daily X posts and YouTube videos
most_recent_content.note: Dr Philips posts regularly on X about liver health, supplement dangers, and alternative medicine myths. No formal blog or changelog.

competitors.what_makes_them_different: N/A — this is an individual clinician. No comparison page exists.

competitors.tagline: Advancing Brain and Nervous System Science
competitors.about: Published by the American Medical Association (AMA). Covers brain science including dementia, traumatic brain injury, stroke, and more. Impact Factor: 21.4 (2024). One of the highest-ranked neurology journals globally.

plans.name: Individual Subscription (Print + Online)
top_features: Full online access to current and past issues

plans.name: Open Access (Author APC)
top_features: Immediate open access to published article

plans.name: Institutional Subscription
plans.price_display: Custom — contact AMA/Ovid for site license pricing
top_features: Unlimited access for all institution members

top_5_features: Impact Factor 21.4 — top-tier neurology journal
Rigorous peer review by the JAMA Network
Original investigations, reviews, and clinical trials
Global perspective from leading neurological research centers
Multimedia content including podcasts and video summaries
competitors.target_customer: Academic researchers, neurologists, clinicians, medical institutions, and libraries

most_recent_post.title: Incidence and Prevalence of Dementia With Lewy Bodies
most_recent_post.summary: Original investigation examining the epidemiological burden of dementia with Lewy bodies, published as an online-first article ahead of print.

competitors.what_makes_them_different: As part of the JAMA Network consortium of 13 peer-reviewed journals, JAMA Neurology benefits from the AMA's editorial infrastructure and brand authority. Key differentiators: extremely high selectivity (low acceptance rate), top-tier impact factor (21.4), and integration with JAMA Network's broader clinical content ecosystem.

competitors.tagline: The most widely read and highly cited peer-reviewed neurology journal
competitors.about: Official journal of the American Academy of Neurology (AAN). Publishes 24 issues per year. Covers all aspects of neurology research and clinical practice. Impact Factor: 9 (2024). Celebrating 75 years of publication in 2026.

plans.name: AAN Member Subscription
plans.price_display: Included with AAN membership (membership dues vary by category)
top_features: Print + online access to Neurology journal

plans.name: Non-Member Individual (Print + Online)
top_features: Online access to full article text

top_5_features: The most widely read and highly cited peer-reviewed neurology journal
Official journal of the American Academy of Neurology (AAN)
75-year publishing history and established brand
Short-form article format introduced in 2018 for faster publication
Integrated CME available through AAN Online Learning Center
competitors.target_customer: Neurologists, neuroscience researchers, AAN members, academic institutions, and hospital libraries

most_recent_post.title: Characterizing Heterogeneity in Brain Morphology in Traumatic Brain Injury Using Normative Modeling
most_recent_post.summary: A study using normative modeling to characterize brain morphology heterogeneity in patients with traumatic brain injury, published as an advance online article.

competitors.what_makes_them_different: As the official journal of the American Academy of Neurology (AAN), Neurology has a built-in subscriber base of ~40,000 members. Key differentiators: 75-year legacy, 'most widely read' claim in the specialty, short-form article model for faster turnaround, and deep integration with AAN clinical practice guidelines and education.
